commands.review_summary:
The branch prints the Gemini CLI OAuth authorization URL before launching the browser and adds a local-login regression test for log-before-open ordering.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction. Current main waits on `ctx.openUrl(authUrl)` and only logs the URL from the catch path, while the linked Windows issue describes a resolved-but-ineffective opener that leaves the user waiting for the callback with no URL to copy.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer validation and merge decision for an already-scoped PR.

target.title: fix(google): print Gemini OAuth URL before browser launch
target.body:
## Summary
- Print the Gemini CLI OAuth authorization URL before attempting best-effort browser launch.
- Keep the existing local callback/browser launch flow intact, but avoid deadlocking users when `openUrl` resolves without actually opening a browser.
- Add a focused regression test for the local browser flow.

Fixes #70964

## Test plan
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/google/oauth.local-login.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/google/oauth.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/google/oauth.ts extensions/google/oauth.local-login.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/google/oauth.ts extensions/google/oauth.local-login.test.ts`
target.author: chen-zhang-cs-code
